page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16 GB of system memory, providing 4 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests placing in the middle 50%. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for multi-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with both read and write operations on large blocks categorized in the bottom 25%. Despite these limitations, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in cryptographic and integer operations, yielding average performance in encryption and single-threaded compression tests.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of a high-speed 402 GB local NVMe SSD provides a solid storage-to-compute value for workloads requiring fast local scratch space without the overhead of detached network storage. Given the moderate single-core capabilities and lower multi-threaded and memory bandwidth efficiency, this server is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, light text processing, and small-scale database operations that benefit from local SSD storage but do not demand intensive parallel computation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16 GB of RAM, and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD. While single-core CPU benchmarks and static web serving throughput register in the average tier, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth fall into the lower performance quartile. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which aid in cryptographic and integer operations. Qualitatively, the server offers a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring fast local storage but moderate parallel compute. It is best suited for static web serving, light database tasks, and general-purpose applications.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and a bundled high-speed local NVMe SSD for storage-dependent workloads.
